title: Bytes, Text I/O, Encodings, Newlines, and Cleanup · Transfer Build
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers bytes, text i/o, encodings, newlines, and cleanup into a changed explicit text-I/O boundary. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of explicit text-I/O boundary with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.
Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.
Select strict rejection, replacement, ignore, or surrogate handling from the stakeholder evidence duty and expose data loss.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Bytes Text Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 9316 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Context Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-context-cleanup-1j1hc3" that uses case 5009 to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Context Cleanup.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Context Cleanup into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Context Cleanup, keep Context Cleanup connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-context-cleanup-1j1hc3
# Competency: bookbot-context-cleanup.
# Case 5009: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_context_cleanup_1j1hc3(byte_count: int = 120, decoded_characters: int = 112, encoding: str = "utf-8", decode_errors: int = 0, handle_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1j1hc3 = "1j1hc3-3ch1j1-5009"
    assert evidence_variant_1j1hc3
    evidence_axes_1j1hc3 = (
        "corpus1j1hc3",
        "policy3ch1j1",
        "revision5009",
        "command1j1hc35009",
        "report3ch1j15009",
        "test50091j1hc3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1j1hc3)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_characters < 0:
        return False, byte_count, "io-count"
    if encoding.casefold() != "utf-8":
        return False, decoded_characters, "encoding-policy"
    if decode_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors, "decode-rejected"
    if not handle_closed:
        return False, byte_count, "cleanup"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_characters
    return True, decoded_characters, "decoded-text"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Context Cleanup structural check is only one gate. explicit text-I/O boundary must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Bookbot and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Context Cleanup
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 9646 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.
